pages.text:
POWER

WHEREAS the Agreement concerning the Inter- national Institute of Refrigeration, replacing the .

Convention of 2lst June, 1920, as modified on 31st

May, 1937, is open for signature at Paris;

WHEREAS the Union of South Africa wishes to participate in the activities of the International

Institute of Refrigeration as a Member Country in the

Sixth Category;

AND WHEREAS it is expedient that a fit and proper person be invested with full power to sign, on behalf of the Government of the Union of South Africa, the abovementioned Agreement;

NOW, THEREFORE, I, ERIC HENDRIK IOUW,

Minister of External Affairs of the Union of South

Africa, do hereby certify and declare that the

Government of the Union of South Africa have autho- rised and appointed, and do by these presents autho- rise and appoint

HARRY THOMSON ANDREWS

to be their plenipotentiary with full power to sign the aforesaid Agreement on behalf of the Government of the Union of South Africa.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF I have signed and sealed these presents at Cape Iown on this the twentieth day of May in the Year of Our Lord One thousand Nine hundred and Fifty-five.
